channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Bend the web to your will with this page-transforming power tool
item.description: Modern web pages aren’t exactly what you’d call “minimal.” All text aside, they’re almost always overflowing with images, ads, and other extraneous elements.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: Fujifilm GFX100RF Review: A Niche but Exciting Prospect
item.description: Fujifilm's new camera, the GFX100RF, boasts a 100MP GFX sensor and a unique fixed lens design. Will it appeal to photographers looking for a specialist experience?
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Unlock the Power of Apple Intelligence on Your iPhone
item.description: Discover exciting features released with iOS 18.1, including smart summaries for emails and messages, epic photo editing, and Siri's natural language queries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Canadian news outlets sue OpenAI over copyright infringement
item.description: Five leading Canadian news outlets filed a lawsuit against OpenAI on Friday, accusing the ChatGPT owner of violating copyright laws to train its artificial intelligence (AI) models.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: Canada’s antitrust watchdog is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australian Senate debates social media ban for under-16s
item.description: The major parties' support for the ban all but guarantees the legislation will become law. But many child welfare and mental health advocates are concerned about unintended consequences.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Gov. Newsom sets himself up as a foil to Elon Musk and Donald Trump
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- As Elon Musk prepares to wield more power in Washington, the state where he built his fortune is girding for a fight.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal investigators in New York are seeking records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed this summer in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Shining a light on shadow AI: Three ways to keep your enterprise safe
item.description: To mitigate shadow AI, leaders should build cross-functional teams to leverage interdisciplinary expertise and foster organizational awareness. The post Shining a light on shadow AI: Three ways to keep your enterprise safe first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How to (kindly) ask people not to post your kids' photos on social media
item.description: With kids facing a seemingly increasing amount of threats from creeps on the internet, here's how you can respectfully ask people not to post photos of your children on social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This TikTok user says a popular YouTube music channel is in fact AI
item.description: Are you really a student if you’ve not done a late night study session soundtracked to an unending lo-fi beats playlist on YouTube? According to Google Trends, searches for the term “lo-fi” on YouTube have tripled since 2020.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: 10 ways AI can make farmers’ jobs easier
item.description: For those who work the earth, AI can be an invaluable tool for making that work more efficient, sustainable and productive. AI helps farmers use the same tools they have been using for centuries, but with improved efficiency and focus.
item.dc:creator: Richard Baguley, Fast Company

item.title: Linux command line for beginners: 25 essential commands
item.description: Getting started on the Linux command line might seem overwhelming at first, but the many commands you need to use will fall into place more quickly than you might imagine. Let’s start with some introductory but very important commands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OPM tries a shift-left approach to anticipating the costs of new tech projects
item.description: When thinking of new technology projects, OPM officials are trying to get a handler on potential costs. The post OPM tries a shift-left approach to anticipating the costs of new tech projects first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Interpol clamps down on cybercrime and arrests over 1,000 suspects in Africa
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Interpol arrested 1,006 suspects in Africa during a massive two-month operation, clamping down on cybercrime that left tens of thousands of victims, including some who were trafficked, and produced millions in financial damages, the global police organization said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Build generative AI pipelines without the infrastructure headache
item.description: While creating a basic ChatGPT prototype might take a weekend, developing production-ready generative AI systems that securely handle enterprise data presents significantly greater engineering challenges.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: NARA to launch AI tool to improve customer experience
item.description: Sheena Burrell, who was NARA's chief information officer until Nov. 21, said the agency is upgrading systems to deliver a modernized user experience. The post NARA to launch AI tool to improve customer experience first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Rethinking defense technology: A blueprint for US success in a complex global landscape
item.description: The Defense Department has made efforts to enhance its AI capabilities through the AI Adoption Strategy. The post Rethinking defense technology: A blueprint for US success in a complex global landscape first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Is Outlook down? Thousands of Microsoft 365 users report outage issues
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Thousands of Microsoft 365 customers worldwide reported having issues with services like Outlook and Teams on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Orbia streamlines security across global sites with SASE
item.description: A push for greater network visibility and global security standards drove Orbia, an $8.2 billion company that operates in more than 100 countries, to deploy secure access service edge (SASE) technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Speed is the killer app
item.description: Why do enterprises so often choose to run applications in the cloud even when it may not be the cheapest option? Why do they turn to open source even if it’s not the most feature-complete choice? And why is generative AI so frothy hot even though, as my InfoWorld colleague David Linthicum argues, “C
item.dc:creator: Info World
